title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary:
The reviewer identified two bootstrap ordering issues in the FluxCD deployment manifests:

1. **Split the ClusterIssuer out of `cert-manager.yaml`** — it's bundled with the HelmRelease, so on a fresh cluster `kubectl apply -k` tries to create it before the cert-manager CRDs exist (async install), causing a guaranteed first-apply failure. It should be moved into a separate FluxCD Kustomization with a `dependsOn` on cert-manager.

2. **Add explicit Namespace resources** — the kustomization references five namespaces that don't exist on a fresh cluster, and `install.createNamespace: true` only helps helm-controller, not the initial `kubectl apply`. A `namespaces.yaml` with all required Namespace objects should be added to the kustomization's resource list before everything else.

code_comments.body:
**ClusterIssuer will fail on initial kustomization apply**

The `ClusterIssuer` is bundled in the same file as the `HelmRelease`, so when `kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/` is run (the documented deploy command), both documents are applied simultaneously. At that moment, the `cert-manager.io/v1` CRD doesn't exist yet — it only gets created after FluxCD's helm-controller finishes installing the cert-manager Helm chart, which is an asynchronous operation that happens after the HelmRelease CR is processed.

Kubernetes will reject the `ClusterIssuer` apply with:
```
no matches for kind "ClusterIssuer" in version "cert-manager.io/v1"
```

The reference docs acknowledge this but attribute incorrect FluxCD behaviour (docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md line 111–112 says "the HelmRelease installs CRDs first, making the ClusterIssuer valid" — this is not true for `kubectl apply -k`; the HelmRelease install is asynchronous). FluxCD will retry and the resource will eventually converge, but the first reconciliation will always fail in a freshly-bootstrapped cluster, which is noisy and delays the initial deployment.

The standard FluxCD pattern for post-install CRD-dependent resources is to separate them into their own FluxCD Kustomization CR with a `dependsOn` on the cert-manager HelmRelease, or to use a `wait: true` health-check gate.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: deploy/flux-system/releases/cert-manager.yaml
Line: 32-38

Comment:
**ClusterIssuer will fail on initial kustomization apply**

The `ClusterIssuer` is bundled in the same file as the `HelmRelease`, so when `kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/` is run (the documented deploy command), both documents are applied simultaneously. At that moment, the `cert-manager.io/v1` CRD doesn't exist yet — it only gets created after FluxCD's helm-controller finishes installing the cert-manager Helm chart, which is an asynchronous operation that happens after the HelmRelease CR is processed.

Kubernetes will reject the `ClusterIssuer` apply with:
```
no matches for kind "ClusterIssuer" in version "cert-manager.io/v1"
```

The reference docs acknowledge this but attribute incorrect FluxCD behaviour (docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md line 111–112 says "the HelmRelease installs CRDs first, making the ClusterIssuer valid" — this is not true for `kubectl apply -k`; the HelmRelease install is asynchronous). FluxCD will retry and the resource will eventually converge, but the first reconciliation will always fail in a freshly-bootstrapped cluster, which is noisy and delays the initial deployment.

The standard FluxCD pattern for post-install CRD-dependent resources is to separate them into their own FluxCD Kustomization CR with a `dependsOn` on the cert-manager HelmRelease, or to use a `wait: true` health-check gate.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: The ClusterIssuer was removed from deploy/flux-system/releases/cert-manager.yaml (which now contains only the HelmRelease) and extracted into a new file deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/cluster-issuer.yaml. A new deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml was created as a separate kustomize overlay listing cluster-issuer.yaml, mariadb.yaml, and memcached.yaml — all CRD-dependent resources that must be applied after operators install their CRDs. The deployment instructions in docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md were rewritten to a two-step process (step 1: kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/, step 2: kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/) and the incorrect claim that 'the HelmRelease installs CRDs first, making the ClusterIssuer valid' was removed and replaced with accurate guidance. The same CRD-separation pattern was applied to the MariaDB and Memcached infrastructure CRs (deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml, deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/memcached.yaml) which were moved out of the base kustomization into the infrastructure kustomization, since they have the identical CRD-dependency problem.

code_comments.body:
**Missing Namespace resources cause apply failure on fresh clusters**

The kustomization deploys resources into five namespaces (`cert-manager`, `mariadb-system`, `external-secrets`, `memcached-system`, and `openstack`) that do not exist in a freshly-bootstrapped cluster (one that only has FluxCD installed). Kubernetes requires a namespace to already exist before any resource can be created in it, so running the documented command `kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/` will produce errors like:

```
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "cert-manager" not found
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "mariadb-system" not found
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "external-secrets" not found
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "memcached-system" not found
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "openstack" not found
```

The `install.createNamespace: true` setting on each HelmRelease only instructs FluxCD's helm-controller to create the namespace when it installs the chart — it does not help the Kustomization controller apply the HelmRelease CR itself (a chicken-and-egg situation). The `openstack` namespace, where the MariaDB and Memcached instance CRs live, has no creation mechanism at all.

The fix is to add `Namespace` resources to the kustomization. A common approach is a single `namespaces.yaml` file:
```yaml
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: cert-manager
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: mariadb-system
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: external-secrets
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: memcached-system
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: openstack
```
Then add `- namespaces.yaml` before the other entries in the `resources` list so kustomize (which orders Namespace resources first) ensures they exist before everything else.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml
Line: 18-34

Comment:
**Missing Namespace resources cause apply failure on fresh clusters**

The kustomization deploys resources into five namespaces (`cert-manager`, `mariadb-system`, `external-secrets`, `memcached-system`, and `openstack`) that do not exist in a freshly-bootstrapped cluster (one that only has FluxCD installed). Kubernetes requires a namespace to already exist before any resource can be created in it, so running the documented command `kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/` will produce errors like:

```
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "cert-manager" not found
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "mariadb-system" not found
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "external-secrets" not found
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "memcached-system" not found
Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "openstack" not found
```

The `install.createNamespace: true` setting on each HelmRelease only instructs FluxCD's helm-controller to create the namespace when it installs the chart — it does not help the Kustomization controller apply the HelmRelease CR itself (a chicken-and-egg situation). The `openstack` namespace, where the MariaDB and Memcached instance CRs live, has no creation mechanism at all.

The fix is to add `Namespace` resources to the kustomization. A common approach is a single `namespaces.yaml` file:
```yaml
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: cert-manager
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: mariadb-system
---

metadata:
  name: external-secrets
---
apiVersion: v1

metadata:
  name: memcached-system
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: openstack
```
Then add `- namespaces.yaml` before the other entries in the `resources` list so kustomize (which orders Namespace resources first) ensures they exist before everything else.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: A new file deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml was created containing all 5 Namespace resources (cert-manager, mariadb-system, external-secrets, memcached-system, openstack) exactly as suggested. It was added as the first entry in deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml's resources list (line 21: '- namespaces.yaml') so kustomize's resource ordering ensures namespaces are created before any namespaced resources. The kustomization.yaml header comment was updated to reference the namespace resources, and docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md was updated with a new 'Namespaces' section explaining the chicken-and-egg problem and why explicit Namespace resources are needed alongside install.createNamespace: true. The design decisions section was also updated to document this as 'Explicit namespace resources'.

resolution_summary: Both review comments were addressed by restructuring the deployment into a two-phase kustomization model: a base kustomization (namespaces, HelmRepository sources, HelmRelease operators) and a separate infrastructure kustomization for CRD-dependent resources. A new namespaces.yaml was added with all 5 namespace resources, the ClusterIssuer was extracted from releases/cert-manager.yaml into its own file at infrastructure/cluster-issuer.yaml, and the MariaDB/Memcached CRs were also moved into the infrastructure kustomization since they have the same CRD-dependency problem as the ClusterIssuer. The reference documentation (docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md) was substantially rewritten to reflect the two-phase deployment, correct the previously inaccurate FluxCD behavior claims, and document the new directory layout.
